title: MusicX
tagline: Offline music player focused on speed and simplicity.
description: Offline music player focused on speed and simplicity.
longDescription: MusicX is an offline music player I built because I was tired of ads and bloated apps. It plays your local files, makes playlists, and just works. No accounts, no internet needed. Working on a v2 with some new ideas.

Core


Overview

Info
wiki.content:
No ads. No subscriptions. No accounts.
No Start Free Trial button stalking you.

Why I Built This

Most music apps are doing the absolute most.

Ads.
Premium popups.
Sign in to continue.
Upgrade to unlock basic functionality.

I just wanted to press play and hear music.

So I made MusicX.

It opens.
It scans your songs.
It plays them.

What You Get

  • Fully offline playback
  • Local music scanning
  • Fast search for songs, artists, and metadata
  • Playlist support
  • Lyrics support through embedded lyrics and LRCLIB
  • Background playback
  • Lock screen and notification controls
  • Clean, minimal UI
  • Advanced color customization

No streaming.
No cloud.
No tracking.
No account creation side quest.

Customization

You can tweak pretty much every color in the app — backgrounds, text, accents, player UI, buttons, and more.

If you want your music player pure black and minimal, you can.

If you want it looking like a neon science experiment, you can also do that.

How It Works

  • Scans your music using Android MediaStore
  • Everything stays on your device
  • Built with Kotlin and Jetpack Compose
  • Uses Media3 / ExoPlayer for playback

FAQ

Does it stream music?
No. Local files only.

Does it need internet?
Nope.

Are there ads?
Never.

Do I need an account?
Absolutely not.

Is my music uploaded anywhere?
No. It stays on your device.

The Goal

Open app, pick song, play music.

That's it.

No popups.
No paywalls.
No emotional manipulation to buy premium.

docs:
# MusicX Documentation

> **MusicX** — Free, open-source, offline music player for Android.
> *No ads. No subscriptions. No accounts. Your music should stay yours.*

## Getting Started

### Introduction

MusicX is an Android music player built with simplicity and privacy in mind. Unlike commercial streaming apps, MusicX runs entirely offline and does not collect any data, require accounts, or show advertisements. It scans your device for audio files and provides a clean, modern interface powered by Jetpack Compose and Material 3.

**Key highlights:**
- 100% offline — no internet required for core playback
- No ads, no subscriptions, no sign-up walls
- Synced lyrics with line-by-line highlighting
- 7 home screen widgets for quick access
- Deep theme customization with 60+ color properties
- Audio effects: bass boost, surround sound, volume normalization
- Import music from local files or download from YouTube
- Background playback with media notification controls
- Automatic metadata correction via MusicBrainz

**Package:** `com.example.musicx`
**Current Version:** 1.6.20
**Developer:** YummyFiles
**Repository:** https://github.com/yummyfiles/MusicX

---

### Installation

#### From Pre-built APK

1. Download the latest APK from the [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/yummyfiles/MusicX/releases/latest) page.
2. Enable **Install from unknown sources** in your Android settings if prompted.
3. Open the downloaded APK file and follow the on-screen prompts to install.

#### Building from Source

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/yummyfiles/MusicX.git
cd MusicX/MusicX

# Build a debug APK
./gradlew assembleDebug

# Build a release APK (requires keystore at MusicX/keystore/release.jks)
./gradlew assembleRelease
```

The signed release APK will be generated at:
```
app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk
```

#### F-Droid (Planned)

MusicX is being prepared for inclusion in the F-Droid repository. The GPLv3 license and F-Droid metadata have been added for compliance.

---

### Requirements

| Requirement | Details |
|-------------|---------|
| **Android Version** | 7.0 (Nougat, API 24) or higher |
| **Target SDK** | 35 (Android 15) |
| **Storage** | Minimal — app data only. Imported files stored internally. |
| **Permissions** | Storage access (for music scanning), Notifications (for playback), Internet (for lyrics/metadata only) |
| **Build Requirements** | JDK 17+, Android SDK (compileSdk 37), Gradle |

---

### Quick Start

1. **Install** MusicX on your Android device.
2. **Grant storage permission** when prompted — this allows MusicX to scan your music library.
3. **Browse your library** on the Songs tab. All audio files longer than 30 seconds are automatically listed.
4. **Tap a song** to start playing. Use the mini player at the bottom to control playback.
5. **Long-press a song** to access options like add to playlist, edit metadata, or delete.
6. **Swipe up on the mini player** to open the full-screen player with album art and lyrics.

---

## Using MusicX

### Your Library

MusicX automatically scans your device for audio files using the Android MediaStore. The library includes:

- **Songs** — All detected audio tracks, sorted by title by default
- **Artists** — Songs grouped by artist name
- **Albums** — Songs grouped by album name
- **Genres** — Songs grouped by genre tag

**Library scanning behavior:**
- Files shorter than 30 seconds are excluded by default (configurable in Library Settings)
- The library auto-refreshes on app startup (configurable)
- Files in `.nomedia` folders are ignored by default
- Imported files (via the Import tab) are stored in app-internal storage and persist across scans

**Song display:**
- Album art is shown when available (embedded art preferred by default)
- Currently playing song is highlighted with a glow effect and accent border
- Song duration is displayed alongside the title
- Long-press any song for a context menu

**Importing music:**
- Use the **Import** tab to add audio files from your device's file system
- Supports the Android Storage Access Framework (SAF) for secure file picking
- Persisted read access ensures imported files remain available after app restarts

**Browsing:**
- Tap on any artist, album, or genre to see a filtered list of songs
- Navigate back using the system back gesture or the back arrow

---

### Playing Music

MusicX uses Android Media3 (ExoPlayer) for high-quality audio playback.

**Basic controls:**
| Action | How |
|--------|-----|
| Play/Pause | Tap the play/pause button on the mini player or full player |
| Next track | Swipe left on the full player, or tap the next button |
| Previous track | Swipe right on the full player, or tap the previous button |
| Seek | Drag the progress bar on the mini player or full player |
| Shuffle | Tap the shuffle icon (crossed arrows) to randomize play order |
| Repeat | Tap the repeat icon to cycle: Off → Repeat One → Repeat All |

**Mini Player:**
The persistent bottom bar shows:
- Album art thumbnail
- Song title and artist
- Play/pause and next buttons
- Progress indicator

Tap the mini player to open the full-screen Now Playing screen.

**Now Playing Screen:**
- Large album art display
- Song title and artist
- Synced lyrics (when available)
- Full transport controls: previous, rewind 10s, play/pause, forward 10s, next
- Progress bar with timestamps
- Shuffle and repeat toggles

**Playback features:**
- **Autoplay** — Automatically plays the next track when the current one ends
- **Gapless playback** — Seamless transitions between tracks (in development)
- **Position memory** — Optionally save and restore playback position for long tracks (podcasts, audiobooks)
- **Jump to Now Playing** — Optionally open the full player immediately when a song is selected

---

### Playlists

MusicX includes a built-in playlist system stored in a local Room database.

**Creating a playlist:**
1. Go to the **Playlists** tab
2. Tap the **+** button
3. Enter a name for the playlist
4. Tap **Create**

**Adding songs to a playlist:**
1. Long-press a song in the library or search results
2. Select **Add to Playlist**
3. Choose the target playlist

**Managing playlists:**
- **Rename** — Tap the edit icon next to a playlist name
- **Delete** — Long-press or swipe to delete a playlist
- **Remove songs** — Open the playlist and remove individual tracks

**Playlist screen:**
- Lists all created playlists with song counts
- Tap a playlist to see its contents
- Songs in playlists play in order (shuffle can be toggled)

---

### Search

The search feature provides real-time filtering across your entire music library.

**How to search:**
1. Tap the **Search** tab in the bottom navigation
2. Type a query — results update as you type (debounced)

**Filter tabs:**
| Tab | Searches |
|-----|----------|
| All | Songs, artists, and albums |
| Songs | Song titles only |
| Artists | Artist names only |
| Albums | Album names only |

**Search tips:**
- Search works across titles, artist names, and album names
- Results are highlighted as you type
- Tap a result to start playing immediately
- The search index updates when the library is refreshed

---

### Queue

The playback queue determines the order in which songs play.

**Queue behavior:**
- When you tap a song, it starts playing and the remaining songs in the current list form the queue
- **Shuffle** randomizes the queue order while keeping the current song playing
- **Repeat One** loops the current track
- **Repeat All** loops the entire queue
- **Autoplay** continues to the next song when the current one finishes

**Managing the queue:**
- The queue is built from the current context (library view, playlist, album, artist, or search results)
- Switching to a different song context replaces the queue
- Shuffle toggling reorders the remaining queue without interrupting playback

---

### Downloads

MusicX includes a YouTube audio downloader for importing music from online sources.

**How to download:**
1. Go to the **Import** tab
2. Paste a YouTube URL in the input field
3. Tap **Download**
4. The audio is extracted and saved to your local library

**Download pipeline:**
MusicX uses a multi-tier failover system to maximize reliability:

| Tier | Source | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| 1 | Piped API | Privacy-focused YouTube frontend (6 fallback instances) |
| 2 | Invidious API | Alternative YouTube frontend (4 fallback instances) |
| 3 | Custom yt-audio-api | User-hosted Flask-based converter (configurable URL) |

**Configuration:**
- In **Library Settings**, you can set a custom `yt-audio-api` URL if you self-host the converter
- The app automatically tries each tier and falls back if one fails

**Requirements:**
- Internet connection
- The `INTERNET` permission is required

---

### Lyrics

MusicX supports synced (line-by-line) and unsynced (plain text) lyrics.

**Lyrics sources (in priority order):**

| Priority | Source | Type |
|----------|--------|------|
| 1 | Local `.lrc` sidecar file | Synced |
| 2 | LRCLIB API | Synced |
| 3 | LRCLIB API | Unsynced/plain text |

**Synced lyrics:**
- Lines highlight in real-time as the song plays
- 150ms prediction offset compensates for Bluetooth audio latency
- LRC `offset` tags are supported for manual timing adjustments
- The lyrics screen auto-scrolls to keep the current line centered

**Lyrics settings:**
| Setting | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Show Lyrics in Player | Auto-fetch and display lyrics on the Now Playing screen |
| Synced Lyrics | Enable time-synced line highlighting |
| Bigger Lyrics | Use larger text for readability |
| Center Lyrics | Center-align lyrics text |

**Nerdcore / Independent support:**
- Smart metadata inversion detection (swaps artist/title when tags are reversed)
- Featured artist extraction from titles (e.g., "Song (feat. Artist)")
- Bracket and parentheses sanitization for improved lyrics matching
- Multi-tier search pipeline with 5 fallback strategies

**Editing lyrics:**
- Long-press a song → **Edit Metadata** → enter custom lyrics
- Custom lyrics are stored locally and override fetched lyrics

---

### Widgets

MusicX provides 7 home screen widgets built with Jetpack Glance.

| Widget | Size | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| **Mini Player** | Small | Compact player with song info and play/pause |
| **Medium Player** | Medium | Larger player with album art and full controls |
| **Large Album** | Large | Full album art display with transport controls |
| **Favorites** | Small/Medium | Quick-play your liked songs |
| **Playlist Launcher** | Small/Medium | Jump directly into a playlist |
| **Quick Actions** | Small | Shuffle, next, and previous buttons |
| **Shuffle** | Small | One-tap shuffle play of entire library |

**Widget features:**
- Auto-update when playback state changes
- Album art display (when available)
- Background refresh via `WidgetUpdateManager`
- Broadcast receiver for system-wide playback updates

**Adding a widget:**
1. Long-press on your home screen
2. Select **Widgets**
3. Find **MusicX** in the list
4. Drag the desired widget to your home screen

---

### Settings

MusicX offers extensive configuration organized into several categories.

#### Playback Settings

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| Autoplay Next | On | Automatically play the next track |
| Jump to Now Playing | Off | Open full player when a song is selected |
| Pause on Disconnect | On | Pause when headphones are removed |
| Gapless Playback | On | Remove silence between tracks (WIP) |
| Remember Position | Off | Save/restore position for long tracks |
| Fade on Play/Pause | On | Smooth volume ramping (200ms) |

#### Library Settings

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| Auto Refresh | On | Scan for new files on startup |
| Exclude Small Files | On | Hide audio shorter than 30 seconds |
| Prefer Embedded Art | On | Use album art embedded in audio files |
| Ignore .nomedia | Off | Scan .nomedia folders (WIP) |
| YT Converter URL | *(empty)* | Custom yt-audio-api endpoint |

#### Lyrics Settings

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| Show Lyrics in Player | On | Auto-fetch and display lyrics |
| Synced Lyrics | On | Time-synced line highlighting |
| Romanized Lyrics | Off | Pronunciation for non-Latin scripts (WIP) |
| Bigger Lyrics | Off | Larger text for readability |
| Center Lyrics | On | Center-align lyrics text |
| Genius API Key | *(empty)* | Optional Genius API key for lyrics |

#### Playback Settings (Advanced)

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| Equalizer | Off | Master equalizer toggle |
| Bass Boost | Off | Enhanced low-frequency response |
| Surround Sound | Off | Virtual spatial audio effect |
| Normalization | On | Equalize volume across songs (85% cap) |
| Smart Gain | Off | Auto-adjust input levels (WIP) |

#### Customize Tabs

| Tab | Default | Notes |
|-----|---------|-------|
| Songs | Visible | Can be hidden |
| Playlists | Visible | Can be hidden |
| Import | Visible | Can be hidden |
| Search | Visible | Can be hidden |
| Settings | Visible | **Always visible** — cannot be hidden |

#### Other Settings

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| Show Splash | On | Show animated splash screen on launch |
| In-App Updates | — | Check GitHub for new versions |

---

## Customization

### Themes

MusicX features a powerful theming system with 60+ individually customizable color properties.

**Theme categories (13 total):**

| Category | Properties | Description |
|----------|------------|-------------|
| Backgrounds | 10 colors | Screen backgrounds, cards, surfaces |
| Text | 5 colors | Primary, secondary, disabled text |
| Borders & Outlines | 5 colors | Dividers, outlines, strokes |
| Accents | 5 colors | Primary accent, highlights |
| Buttons | 6 colors | Primary, secondary, disabled button states |
| Icons | 4 colors | Active, inactive, accent icons |
| Sliders & Progress | 5 colors | Seek bars, progress indicators |
| Toggles | 3 colors | Switch track, thumb, active state |
| Inputs | 5 colors | Text fields, cursors, labels |
| Player Controls | 6 colors | Transport buttons, seek bar |
| Lyrics | 2 colors | Active line, inactive line |
| Navigation | 2 colors | Bottom nav active/inactive |
| Misc | 6 colors | Splash, widgets, misc elements |

**Managing themes:**
1. Go to **Settings → Appearance**
2. Tap a color category to expand it
3. Use the RGBA sliders to adjust each color property
4. Tap **Save Theme** and enter a name
5. Load or delete saved themes from the theme list

**Exporting and importing themes:**
- **Export** — Save your current theme as a JSON file
- **Import** — Load a shared theme from a JSON file
- Share themes with other MusicX users by exchanging JSON files

---

### Appearance

The Appearance settings screen provides control over the visual look of MusicX.

**Available options:**

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Theme Color Picker | Full RGBA sliders for 60+ color properties |
| Named Themes | Save, load, and delete color presets |
| Theme Export/Import | Share themes as JSON files |
| Tab Visibility | Show/hide bottom navigation tabs |
| Splash Screen | Toggle the animated splash screen |

**Tab customization:**
- Navigate to **Settings → Customize Tabs**
- Toggle visibility for: Songs, Playlists, Import, Search
- The Settings tab is protected and always visible

---

### Notifications

MusicX displays a media notification during playback with standard transport controls.

**Notification features:**
- Album art display
- Song title and artist
- Play/pause, next, previous buttons
- Progress indicator
- Swipe to dismiss (stops playback)

**Notification settings:**
- Requires `POST_NOTIFICATIONS` permission on Android 13+
- Can be disabled in system settings (stops background playback)
- The notification is tied to the foreground service

**Foreground service:**
- MusicX runs a foreground service during playback
- This prevents the system from killing the app in the background
- Requires `FOREGROUND_SERVICE` and `FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MEDIA_PLAYBACK` permissions

---

### Audio

MusicX includes several audio processing features.

#### Equalizer

- Toggle the master equalizer from **Settings → Audio**
- Uses the Android system equalizer
- Provides frequency band adjustment

#### Bass Boost

- Enhances low-frequency response
- Toggle from **Settings → Audio**
- Uses the Android `BassBoost` audio effect

#### Surround Sound

- Virtual spatial audio effect
- Toggle from **Settings → Audio**
- Uses the Android `Virtualizer` audio effect

#### Volume Normalization

- Automatically equalizes volume across different songs
- Caps volume at 85% to prevent clipping
- Enabled by default for a consistent listening experience

#### Fade Effects

- Smooth volume ramping on play and pause
- 200ms duration with 20ms step intervals
- Prevents abrupt audio starts and stops
- Configurable from **Settings → Playback**

---

## Advanced

### Privacy

MusicX is designed with privacy as a core principle.

**Privacy guarantees:**
- **No accounts** — MusicX never asks you to sign up or log in
- **No data collection** — No analytics, telemetry, or tracking of any kind
- **No internet required** — Core playback works entirely offline
- **No ads** — Zero advertising of any form
- **No cloud sync** — All data stays on your device
- **Open source** — Fully auditable under GPLv3

**Internet usage (optional):**
MusicX only connects to the internet when you explicitly use these features:
- Lyrics fetching (LRCLIB API)
- Metadata validation (MusicBrainz API)
- YouTube audio downloading (Piped/Invidious APIs)
- In-app update checking (GitHub Releases API)

No personal data is transmitted during any of these operations.

---

### Permissions

| Permission | Required | Purpose |
|------------|----------|---------|
| `READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE` | Yes | Scan device for audio files (Android < 13) |
| `READ_MEDIA_AUDIO` | Yes | Scan device for audio files (Android 13+) |
| `INTERNET` | Optional | Lyrics, metadata validation, YouTube downloads, updates |
| `POST_NOTIFICATIONS` | Optional | Display playback notification (Android 13+) |
| `FOREGROUND_SERVICE` | Required | Background playback service |
| `FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MEDIA_PLAYBACK` | Required | Media-specific foreground service |
| `REQUEST_INSTALL_PACKAGES` | Optional | Install APK updates in-app |

**Notes:**
- Storage permission is required for the app to function
- Notification permission is optional but recommended for background playback
- Internet permission is only needed for online features (lyrics, YouTube, updates)

---

### Storage

**App data storage:**
- Room database: playlists, metadata overrides, liked songs, ignored songs, validated metadata, library songs
- DataStore: settings and theme preferences (stored as JSON)
- Imported audio files: stored in app-internal storage

**Database schema (Room, version 8):**

| Table | Fields | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| `Playlist` | id, name, songUris | User-created playlists |
| `LibrarySong` | uri, title, artist, duration, albumArtUri, lyrics, album, genre | Cached library metadata |
| `MetadataOverride` | songUri, customTitle, customArtist, customLyrics | User-edited metadata |
| `IgnoredSong` | uri | Songs marked as ignored/deleted |
| `LikedSong` | uri | User's favorite songs |
| `ValidatedMetadata` | songUri, originalArtist, originalAlbum, validatedArtist, validatedAlbum, confidence | MusicBrainz-validated metadata |

**File management:**
- Imported files are copied to internal storage and persist
- Deleted songs are added to the ignore list to prevent re-appearing
- Album art is loaded from embedded metadata or device MediaStore

---

### Troubleshooting

#### Songs not appearing in library
- Ensure storage permission is granted
- Check that **Exclude Small Files** setting isn't hiding your tracks
- Try **Auto Refresh** or manually restart the app
- Files in `.nomedia` folders are ignored by default

#### Playback not starting
- Check that the audio file is not corrupted
- Try a different audio file to isolate the issue
- Ensure no other app is hogging the audio focus
- Restart the app

#### Lyrics not showing
- Lyrics require an internet connection for LRCLIB fetching
- Some songs may not have lyrics available
- Check that **Show Lyrics in Player** is enabled in Lyrics Settings
- Try editing the metadata and entering lyrics manually

#### YouTube download failing
- Ensure internet connectivity
- The Piped/Invidious APIs may be temporarily down — the app will try multiple fallback instances
- If using a custom yt-audio-api, verify the URL is correct in Library Settings
- Check that the YouTube URL is valid and the video is public

#### Notification not appearing
- Grant the `POST_NOTIFICATIONS` permission in system settings
- Ensure the notification channel is not disabled in Android settings
- On some devices, battery optimization may need to be disabled for MusicX

#### App crashes or freezes
- Clear the app cache from Android settings
- As a last resort, clear app data (this resets all settings and playlists)
- Report the issue on GitHub with steps to reproduce

#### Widget not updating
- Widgets auto-update on playback state changes
- If stuck, remove and re-add the widget
- Check that battery optimization is disabled for MusicX

---

### FAQ

**Q: Is MusicX really free?**
A: Yes. MusicX is completely free with no in-app purchases, no premium tier, and no ads.

**Q: Does MusicX need internet?**
A: No. Core playback is fully offline. Internet is only needed for optional features like lyrics fetching, metadata validation, and YouTube downloads.

**Q: Where does MusicX store my music?**
A: MusicX reads from your device's MediaStore. Imported files are copied to app-internal storage. No files are uploaded anywhere.

**Q: Can I use MusicX with Bluetooth headphones?**
A: Yes. MusicX includes a 150ms prediction offset for synced lyrics to compensate for Bluetooth audio latency.

**Q: Does MusicX support FLAC, OGG, MP3, and other formats?**
A: MusicX supports any audio format that Android's MediaCodec can decode. This includes MP3, FLAC, OGG, AAC, WAV, M4A, and more.

**Q: Can I export my playlists?**
A: Playlist data is stored in the Room database. Theme data can be exported as JSON. Full playlist export is planned for a future release.

**Q: Is my data sent anywhere?**
A: No. MusicX does not collect, transmit, or sell any user data. Optional network requests (lyrics, metadata) only send non-personal song identifiers.

**Q: Can I run MusicX on a Chromebook?**
A: MusicX is an Android app and may work on Chromebooks with Android app support, but it is primarily designed for phones.

**Q: How do I report a bug?**
A: Open an issue on [GitHub](https://github.com/yummyfiles/MusicX/issues) using the Bug Report template.

---

## Developers

### Building MusicX

#### Prerequisites

| Tool | Version |
|------|---------|
| JDK | 17+ (21 recommended) |
| Android Studio | Latest stable with Compose support |
| Android SDK | compileSdk 37, targetSdk 35, minSdk 24 |
| Gradle | Included via wrapper (Gradle daemon JVM: 21) |

#### Build Commands

```bash
# Clone and enter the project
git clone https://github.com/yummyfiles/MusicX.git
cd MusicX/MusicX

# Debug build
./gradlew assembleDebug

# Release build (requires keystore at keystore/release.jks)
./gradlew assembleRelease

# Run unit tests
./gradlew test

# Run instrumented tests (requires emulator/device)
./gradlew connectedAndroidTest

# Clean build
./gradlew clean
```

#### Build Configuration

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Gradle JVM Heap | 4096 MB |
| Parallel Builds | Enabled |
| Configuration Cache | Enabled |
| Java Source/Target | 11 |
| ProGuard/R8 | Enabled (release) |
| Minification | Enabled (release) |
| Resource Shrinking | Enabled (release) |

#### Output Locations

| Build | Path |
|-------|------|
| Debug APK | `app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk` |
| Release APK | `app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk` |

#### CI/CD

GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/release.yml`):
- Triggers on version tags (`v*`) or manual dispatch
- Uses JDK 17 (Temurin)
- Builds `assembleRelease`
- Uploads APK as artifact
- Creates GitHub Release with auto-generated release notes

---

### Architecture

#### Pattern: MVVM with Jetpack Compose

```
+---------------------------------------------------+
|                    UI Layer                        |
|  +----------+  +----------+  +----------+         |
|  |  Screens  |  |Components|  |  Theme   |         |
|  | (Compose) |  | (Compose)|  | (Compose)|         |
|  +-----+----+  +-----+----+  +----------+         |
|        |              |                            |
|  +-----+----+  +-----+----+                       |
|  |ViewModels|  |  Navigation|                      |
|  |  (Flows) |  | (Nav3)    |                      |
|  +-----+----+  +----------+                       |
|        |                                           |
+--------+-------------------------------------------+
|        |          Domain Layer                     |
|  +-----+--------------------------------+         |
|  |         Repositories                 |         |
|  |  MusicRepository                     |         |
|  |  SettingsRepository                  |         |
|  |  MusicXLyricsRepository              |         |
|  |  MusicBrainzClient                   |         |
|  |  LyricsFetcher                       |         |
|  +-----+--------------------------------+         |
|        |                                           |
+--------+-------------------------------------------+
|        |           Data Layer                      |
|  +-----+----------+  +------------------+         |
|  |   Room DB       |  |   DataStore      |         |
|  |  (SQLite)       |  |  (Preferences)   |         |
|  +----------------+  +------------------+         |
|                                                   |
+---------------------------------------------------+
|               Service Layer                       |
|  +--------------+  +------------------+           |
|  |PlaybackService|  |NotificationProvider|          |
|  |(MediaSession) |  |  (Custom)         |          |
|  +--------------+  +------------------+           |
+---------------------------------------------------+
```

#### Key Components

| Component | File | Purpose |
|-----------|------|---------|
| `MainActivity` | `MainActivity.kt` | App entry point, sets up Compose |
| `MusicXApplication` | `MusicXApplication.kt` | Application class, initializes services |
| `MusicXApp` | `MusicXApp.kt` | Main scaffold with navigation |
| `PlaybackService` | `PlaybackService.kt` | MediaSessionService for audio playback |
| `MusicController` | `MusicController.kt` | Client-side MediaController |
| `MusicRepository` | `MusicRepository.kt` | Main data repository |
| `SettingsRepository` | `SettingsRepository.kt` | DataStore preferences wrapper |
| `MusicXLyricsRepository` | `MusicXLyricsRepository.kt` | Lyrics loading orchestration |
| `LyricsFetcher` | `LyricsFetcher.kt` | LRCLIB API client |
| `MusicBrainzClient` | `MusicBrainzClient.kt` | MusicBrainz metadata API |
| `MetadataValidator` | `MetadataValidator.kt` | Metadata validation/repair |
| `YtAudioFetcher` | `YtAudioFetcher.kt` | YouTube audio downloader |
| `MusicDatabase` | `MusicDatabase.kt` | Room database (version 8) |

#### Tech Stack

| Category | Technology | Version |
|----------|-----------|---------|
| Language | Kotlin | 2.1.10 |
| UI | Jetpack Compose | BOM 2024.09.00 |
| Design System | Material 3 | Compose Material3 |
| Navigation | Navigation3 | 1.0.1 |
| Adaptive Layout | Compose Material3 Adaptive | 1.3.0-beta02 |
| Database | Room | 2.7.0 |
| Preferences | DataStore | 1.1.7 |
| Media Playback | Media3 (ExoPlayer) | 1.10.1 |
| Image Loading | Coil | 2.7.0 |
| Networking | OkHttp | 4.10.0 |
| Serialization | Kotlinx Serialization | 1.9.0 |
| JSON | Moshi | 1.15.2 |
| Coroutines | Kotlinx Coroutines | 1.10.2 |
| Widgets | Glance | 1.1.1 |
| Permissions | Accompanist | 0.37.3 |
| Build | Gradle + Kotlin DSL | AGP 9.2.1 |
| Code Processing | KSP | 2.1.10-1.0.30 |

#### External APIs

| API | URL | Purpose |
|-----|-----|---------|
| LRCLIB | https://lrclib.net | Synced and plain lyrics |
| MusicBrainz | https://musicbrainz.org | Metadata validation (rate-limited: 1 req/s) |
| Piped | Multiple instances | YouTube audio stream extraction |
| Invidious | Multiple instances | YouTube audio stream extraction |
| Custom yt-audio-api | User-configured | Self-hosted YouTube audio converter |
| GitHub Releases | api.github.com | In-app update checking |

---

### Contributing

MusicX welcomes contributions from the community.

#### Getting Started

1. Fork the repository on GitHub
2. Clone your fork locally
3. Create a feature branch: `git checkout -b feature/your-feature`
4. Make your changes
5. Run tests: `./gradlew test`
6. Commit with a descriptive message
7. Push to your fork and open a Pull Request

#### Issue Templates

The project provides structured issue templates:

- **Bug Report** — Describe the issue, reproduction steps, expected vs actual behavior, and severity
- **Feature Request** — Describe the feature, the problem it solves, and how it should work
- **Review** — Share your rating and feedback

#### Code Style

- Follow existing code conventions and patterns
- Use Kotlin idioms and coroutines
- Write composable functions for UI components
- Include unit tests for new functionality
- Keep functions focused and testable

#### Project Structure

```
app/src/main/java/com/example/musicx/
  MainActivity.kt
  MusicXApplication.kt
  model/          # Data classes
  data/           # Repositories, API clients, database
    local/        # Room database, DAOs, entities
  playback/       # Media playback service and controller
  ui/             # Compose screens and components
    navigation/   # Navigation routes and app scaffold
    theme/        # Theme definitions and state
    components/   # Reusable UI components
    songs/        # Song list screen
    playlists/    # Playlist screens
    import/       # File import and YouTube download
    search/       # Search screen
    nowplaying/   # Full player and lyrics screen
    browse/       # Filtered song views
    metadata/     # Metadata editor
    splash/       # Splash screen
    settings/     # All settings screens
  widget/         # Home screen widgets (Glance)
```

---

### Changelog

#### v1.7.0
- Lyrics architecture overhaul: domain-layer LRC parser
- Offline-first lyrics pipeline
- Memory-safe player ticker
- Auto-scrolling lyric screen
- 24 unit tests for lyrics parsing

#### v1.6.20
- Major bug fixes and UX improvements
- Playback rewind fix
- Lyrics toggle fix
- Search UI fix
- F-Droid metadata added

#### v1.6.19
- Miniplayer gradient refinement
- Currently-playing song highlighting with glow and accent border

#### v1.6.18
- Invisible save button fix
- Dialog consistency improvements

#### v1.6.17
- Custom yt-audio-api support
- Configurable API URL in Library Settings
- Improved failover system

#### v1.6.16
- Widget fixes
- Shuffle/repeat fixes
- Fade on play/pause
- Appearance fixes
- Performance optimization

#### v1.6.13
- README with light/dark theme support
- Settings tab protection (cannot be hidden)
- Visualizer improvements

#### v1.6.12
- YouTube downloader JSON parsing crash fix
- Defensive JSON extraction

#### v1.6.11
- Enhanced metadata correction layer
- Featured artist extraction from titles
- Smart inversion detection
- Delimiter parsing improvements

#### v1.6.10
- Nerdcore/independent song sync fix
- Multi-tier search pipeline (5 tiers)
- Regex sanitization for lyrics matching

#### v1.6.9
- Synced offline lyrics pipeline
- Interactive lyrics UI
- 150ms prediction offset for Bluetooth latency
- LRC offset tag support

#### v1.2.1
- Sponsor button added

#### Earlier Versions
- v1.0 through v1.5.0 — Initial releases with core music playback, playlists, search, widgets, and theme customization

*MusicX Documentation — Generated for version 1.6.20*
*Repository: https://github.com/yummyfiles/MusicX*

releases.name: v2.0.0
releases.body:
## v2.0.0

### Highlights
- Added comprehensive documentation (MUSICX_DOCUMENTATION.md)
- Added project hub link to README — documentation, wiki, guides at [yummyfiles.vercel.app/projects/musicx](https://yummyfiles.vercel.app/projects/musicx)
- UI component refactoring: MiniPlayer, MusicXApp, NowPlayingScreen, SearchScreen, SongsScreen, SongsViewModel

### Documentation
- Full documentation covering Getting Started, Using MusicX, Customization, Advanced topics, and Developer guide
- Covers all features: lyrics, widgets, playlists, downloads, themes, audio effects, settings
- Includes architecture overview, tech stack, build instructions, and changelog

### Changes
- Simplified MiniPlayer component
- Refactored navigation in MusicXApp
- Updated NowPlayingScreen and SearchScreen
- Cleaned up SongsViewModel

releases.name: v1.6.20
releases.body:
## What's New

### Bug Fixes
- **Playback freeze**: Fixed songs not playing when switching tracks during fade-out
- **Play/pause flicker**: Optimistic state updates prevent visual glitches
- **MiniPlayer gap**: Removed extra bottom padding causing visual gap
- **Progress bar snap-back**: Added seek lock to prevent scrubbing position jumping
- **Lyrics auto-open**: Lyrics no longer auto-open when opening the player
- **Home layout shift**: Fixed search text shifting after returning from player
- **Empty space below songs list**: Fixed excessive padding at bottom of songs list

### Improvements
- **Heart button**: Added favorite/like toggle on NowPlaying screen
- **Search filters**: Proper Artists and Albums tabs with distinct views
- **Lyrics not found message**: Shows message when lyrics unavailable
- **Repeat button states**: OFF state now visually distinct
- **Settings cleanup**: Unfinished sections hidden under Revamping header
- **Tab icon state**: Settings icon highlights for all sub-screens
- **Back buttons**: Consistent arrow icons instead of text labels
- **Import button**: Download text no longer wraps
- **Queue optimization**: Checks existing queue before rebuilding

releases.name: v1.6.19
releases.body:
# MusicX v1.6.19

### ?? UI Refinements
* **Miniplayer Gradient**: Adjusted the bottom bar gradient to fade smoothly from transparent above the miniplayer to solid background color at the navigation bar, removing the redundant fade below.
* **Song Highlighting**: Currently playing songs are now highlighted with a subtle glow and accent border in the list, making it easier to see what's playing at a glance.

releases.name: v1.6.18
releases.body:
# MusicX v1.6.18

### ?? UI Fixes
* **Visible Save Button**: Fixed an issue where the `Save` button in the playlist creation dialog was black on a black background, making it invisible.
* **Renamed Confirm to Save**: Updated the button text in the playlist dialog to `Save` for better clarity.

### ??? Other Improvements
* **Consistency**: Improved consistency across various dialogs in the app.

releases.name: v1.6.17
releases.body:
# MusicX v1.6.17

### YouTube Converter Fix
* **Added support for `yt-audio-api`**: You can now host your own YouTube audio conversion API for faster and more reliable downloads.
* **Custom API Configuration**: Added a new setting in **Settings > Library** to specify your custom API URL.
* **Improved Failover**: The app will automatically fall back to Piped and Invidious instances if your custom API is not configured or fails.

### Other Improvements
* **License Visibility**: Moved the LICENSE file to the project root for better visibility on GitHub.
* **Under-the-hood fixes**: General stability improvements and code cleanup.

releases.name: v1.6.16 - The Reliability Update
releases.body:
## What's New

### Bug Fixes
- **Install dialog icon**: Fixed the APK installation dialog icon being too large and getting cropped. The icon now displays at the correct size without being cut off.
- **Home screen widgets**: Fixed all 7 Glance widgets (Mini Player, Medium Player, Large Album, Favorites, Playlist Launcher, Quick Actions, Shuffle) being stuck showing perpetual loading spinners. Added timeout protection on state reads and error isolation so one widget failing doesn't break all others.
- **Widget update manager**: Added per-widget error isolation so a failure in one widget type no longer prevents other widgets from updating. Also added missing PlaylistLauncherWidget state updates.
- **Widget broadcast receiver**: Fixed potential broadcast timeout kills by using proper goAsync() with structured coroutine scope.

### Previous Changes
- Shuffle and Repeat buttons now work correctly
- Fade on Play/Pause for smooth audio transitions  
- Appearance settings fixes (Album Placeholder color, Inactive Border, App Icon tint)
- Optimized performance for widget updates and large library browsing

releases.name: v1.6.15 - Smooth Fades and Fixes
releases.body:
﻿## What"s New

Replaced those ugly "..." with a clean fade gradient for long song titles. It looks much better in the library and the player now!

Fixed a bunch of settings that were there but didn"t actually do anything. "Jump to now playing" and library filters now work as expected.

Added a brand new "About" screen so you can see more about MusicX and how it"s built.

Marked some other experimental features as "WIP" so you know what"s still being worked on.

Thanks for all the feedback!

releases.name: v1.6.14 - Metadata Magic
releases.body:
﻿## What"s New

Finally fixed the annoying metadata issue where random songs would show stuff like "GOJO RAP SONG" or "Downloads" as the artist or album.

MusicX now does a much better job validating metadata and can check MusicBrainz when something looks off.

Album names now show up in the library too.

Cleaned up a bunch of smaller bugs and made scanning more reliable.

Thanks for using MusicX.

releases.name: v1.6.13 - Theme and Settings polish
releases.body:
﻿## What"s New

Finally made the README and Wiki look good in both light and dark mode on GitHub!

Also made sure you can"t accidentally lock yourself out of the settings by disabling the tab.

Tweaked the visualizer to look a bit punchier and fixed some text that was hard to read in light mode.

Added some background stuff to automate building APKs so updates come out faster.

releases.name: v1.6.12 - Fixing the downloader
releases.body:
﻿## What"s New

Fixed that annoying crash in the YouTube downloader where some servers would send back weird text before the actual data.

The downloader is now a lot smarter about finding the real data and wont just give up when it sees something unexpected.

Also improved how the app handles different YouTube instances so downloads should be more stable now.

releases.name: v1.6.11 - Better metadata for indie tracks
releases.body:
﻿## What"s New

Added some clever logic to fix songs that have the artist and title swapped, which happens a lot with nerdcore and indie tracks.

The app now also picks up featured artists from the title and uses them to help find the right lyrics.

Made everything more consistent so you"ll see "Artist ft. Someone" correctly in the app.

releases.name: v1.6.10 - Improved lyrics matching
releases.body:
﻿## What"s New

Finally fixed those annoying lyrics retrieval failures for tracks with YouTube-style tags like "[ANIME RAP]" or "(Official Video)".

The new multi-tier search system automatically cleans up titles and artists before searching, so you"re much more likely to get the right lyrics.

It even checks the song duration now to make sure it"s matching the right version of a track!

releases.name: v1.6.9 - Synced lyrics are here!
releases.body:
﻿## What"s New

MusicX now supports synchronized lyrics! You can now watch lyrics follow along with your music with millisecond precision.

The app will automatically try to find and cache high-quality synced lyrics for your library using LRCLIB.

I"ve also spent a lot of time making sure the scrolling is super smooth even on older devices, and that everything stays perfectly in sync whether you"re using headphones or Bluetooth.

releases.name: v1.6.8 - Widget and Lyrics fixes
releases.body:
﻿## What"s New

Fixed the widgets getting stuck on "Loading". They should now update properly when you change songs.

Also fixed lyrics not showing up at all when synced lyrics weren"t available. The app will now fallback to plain text lyrics like it used to.

Added a dedicated Lyrics section in settings to make things easier to find.

releases.name: v1.6.7 - Stability improvements
releases.body:
﻿## What"s New

Just a bunch of small stability fixes and UI tweaks to make the app feel a bit more polished.

Fixed a few rare crashes when rotating the screen while playing music.

releases.name: v1.6.6 - Fixing widgets and installs
releases.body:
﻿## What"s New

Fixed a bunch of annoying technical issues with the widgets not showing up on the home screen.

Also fixed some problems with installing the APK on newer versions of Android.

releases.name: v1.6.5 - Fixing feedback links
releases.body:
﻿## What"s New

Fixed those "No browser available" errors when trying to report a bug or request a feature.

It should now open your favorite browser correctly.

releases.name: v1.6.4 - More stable updates
releases.body:
﻿## What"s New

Made the update downloader much more reliable using OkHttp instead of the system downloader.

Widgets also got some love and should now load their data much faster when you first place them.

releases.name: v1.6.3 - Direct GitHub feedback
releases.body:
﻿## What"s New

Added direct links to report bugs or request features on GitHub.

This makes it much easier for you to help me make MusicX better!

releases.name: v1.6.2 - Smarter YouTube downloads
releases.body:
﻿## What"s New

YouTube downloads are now much easier! I"ve switched to using Piped and Invidious APIs, so you don"t need to configure anything anymore.

Also added some cool new sections in the library so you can browse by artist, album, or genre right from the main screen.

And yes, all those widgets I"ve been working on are now officially working and included in this build.
